title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20230716004855
comment: Great day in the canyon. The log rappel in the begin has a hornet nest in or close to it. Be mindful! Also, a bolt was installed that wasn’t there a month ago when we opened it. Not sure what the point is. It was at the same location as the old redirect for high flow sequence for Lower Davis Falls under the bridge.

title: Conditions:Davis Creek Canyon-20231026064829
comment: Due to recent rainfall, water levels were pretty fun but not overwhelming. All anchors were in good shape, I avoided the bees by using the alt DCL tree. Might have been the last run for 2023, the air temps were quite cool and I def felt the chill towards the end of the canyon even in a dry suit. Amazing place, I can see why everyone goes here, I hope to get out there next season again!! I also did my best to look for trash under the bridge and didn't see anything laying around. I documented this canyon with current conditions and obstacles which I'm sure will change but I will add the vid to the media subpart of this canyon page.

title: Conditions:Fir Creek-20230907195134
comment: Fun canyon with some really scenic rappels. A landslide has occurred at the base of the last rappel, which may provide a new exit, did not explore too far as there are also ground wasps making their home in the dirt. Definitely bring a change of clothes for the hike out if taking the long route.

title: Conditions:Hager Canyon-20210813022406
comment: Ran Hager and Davis today. Quick updates. Log in R1 pool has been removed. Could be useful to check depth or find a new anchor to go in flow. Removed logs after S2 and pool just before the triple slides. Fun day. WATCH OUT FOR THE YELLOW JACKETS ON THE EXIT. There is a nest and they were active. We were fortunate.

title: Conditions:Lower Fulton Creek-20260707004956
comment: I cut out most webbing anchors on obvious jumps. Just meat anchors people down drops who don't want to jump, or use cem/toggle. One 10' drop is technical that we rappelled off the log. There is still an anchor on the last drop which is a technical-ish jump. Theres a bolt, hanger, quicklink, teblyon ring anchor dcr that goes as a slide. (It does look like a slide) Exit is wonderfully great to follow. Will hopefully update the exit track. There are ground wasps on approach and exit. For the approach to stay to the right of the track a bit once you start descending. On the exit, avoid the second steep lose section of the exit trail. I got stung once on the entrance and a teammate for 4 stings on the exit.

canyon: Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)
title: Conditions:Rock Creek (Upper North Fork, South Cascades)-20170830051737
comment: Very low water, just a trickle at most of the falls. Deepest pool was about waist deep, but most were far shallower. Walked through a bee hive on the east side of the creek on the stretch between r3 and r4 and got stung on my legs, arms, and neck. :(

title: Conditions:Wallanding Creek-20230807212745
comment: Lovely pretty canyon with beautiful waterfalls, some fun sporty flow, a couple of short jumps. Got to see a salamander at the very end. It is not done very often, but it definitely deserves more love. Anchors were in great condition. We got to do some fun problem solving in the lower section, which have some short drops but no fixed anchor material. The approach sucks a bit, beware of hornets. Exit went pretty smoothly

title: Conditions:Whitehorse Creek (Olympic Peninsula)-20260708034426
comment: Enjoyable canyon. Not quite as good as Fulton. Starts out a little boring, gets better towards the end. Webbing is in decent condition at all of the anchors. There is a paper wasp nest DCL at the downclimb before the R15 anchor, so treat that with respect.
